task: I’m trying to tighten up a small café I help with and want a simple checklist for the end of the night. What are the main things I should make sure are squared away before we lock up so I don’t miss anything important?

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a simple end-of-night café lockup checklist you can use:

### 1) Cash and payments
- Count the register / till
- Match cash, card, and app sales to the POS
- Note any comps, refunds, voids, or discrepancies
- Put cash in the safe / drop as required

### 2) Food and drink
- Label and date anything being saved
- Toss anything unsafe or out of temp
- Cover and store perishables properly
- Check the fridge, freezer, and display cases are closed and set right

### 3) Cleaning and reset
- Wipe down counters, tables, espresso machine, sinks, and high-touch spots
- Sweep and mop floors
- Empty trash and recycling
- Restock basics for the next shift if needed

### 4) Equipment and utilities
- Turn off or put away equipment that should be off
- Check lights, music, heaters/AC, and any small appliances
- Make sure doors, windows, and bathrooms are secure

### 5) Closing notes
- Write down anything the next shift needs to know
- Flag low stock, broken items, customer issues, or unusual transactions
- Leave a quick note if anything needs follow-up tomorrow

If you want, I can turn this into a **super short 10-item closeout checklist** you can keep on your phone or print out.

user_simulator.text:
Mostly fine. But that’s still a little broad.

I’d want it tighter and in the order you actually do it so nothing gets missed when you’re tired:
- cash first
- food temps / anything to save
- trash and dishes
- wipe down and reset stations
- shut off equipment
- lock doors, windows, bathroom
- final walk-through for tabs, lights, and anything left out
- leave a note on problems / low stock

Also worth adding: check the bathrooms one last time. People leave a mess in there and it gets blamed on whoever closes.

If you want, make it a real 10-step closeout with checkboxes, not just categories.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter **10-step end-of-night café closeout checklist** in the order you’d actually do it:

## End-of-Night Café Closeout

- [ ] **1. Count cash / close out payments**
  - Count register/till
  - Check cash, card, app sales against POS
  - Note any voids, refunds, comps, or discrepancies

- [ ] **2. Save food and check temps**
  - Put away anything being kept
  - Label/date leftovers
  - Check fridge/freezer temps
  - Toss anything unsafe or out of temp

- [ ] **3. Take out trash and clear dishes**
  - Empty trash and recycling
  - Run/stack dishes
  - Clear bus tubs, sinks, and dirty prep areas

- [ ] **4. Wipe down and reset stations**
  - Wipe counters, tables, sinks, and high-touch spots
  - Reset coffee/espresso/bar stations
  - Restock basics for open if needed

- [ ] **5. Shut off equipment**
  - Turn off or power down equipment that should be off
  - Check espresso machine, grinders, ovens, heaters/AC, music, etc.

- [ ] **6. Check bathrooms**
  - Quick final clean
  - Make sure toilet paper, soap, and paper towels are stocked
  - Look for anything left behind or damaged

- [ ] **7. Lock doors and windows**
  - Make sure all entrances are secured
  - Check windows, back doors, and any side access points

- [ ] **8. Final walk-through**
  - Check for open tabs
  - Look for lights left on
  - Make sure nothing is left out, on, or running

- [ ] **9. Put away anything sensitive**
  - Cash dropped / safe secured
  - Keys, phone, tablets, receipts, or paperwork stored away

- [ ] **10. Leave a note**
  - Problems to fix
  - Low stock
  - Broken equipment
  - Customer issues or anything unusual

If you want, I can turn this into a **super short one-page version** that’s even easier to print and tape up near the register.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Tight enough.

The bathroom check belongs in there — people do leave it wrecked and then it’s somehow the closer’s problem.

This version’s actually usable: cash first, food, trash, reset, shut down, lock up, final sweep, note anything weird. That’s the order I’d want too.
